Use this when work is complete, the issue is no longer relevant, or it's a duplicate. The closing comment should explain the resolution or reason for closing. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 1 issue(s) can be closed. Target: *.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"body":{"description":"Closing comment explaining why the issue is being closed and summarizing any resolution, workaround, or conclusion.","type":"string"},"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"issue_number":{"description":"Issue number to close. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 901 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/901). If omitted, closes the issue that triggered this workflow (requires an issue event trigger).","type":["number","string"]},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"},"state_reason":{"description":"The reason for closing the issue. Use 'COMPLETED' for resolved issues, 'NOT_PLANNED' for issues that won't be addressed, or 'DUPLICATE' for duplicate issues. Defaults to 'COMPLETED'.","enum":["COMPLETED","NOT_PLANNED","DUPLICATE"],"type":"string"}},"required":["body"],"type":"object"},"name":"close_issue"},{"description":"Add a comment to an existing GitHub issue, pull request, or discussion. Use this to provide feedback, answer questions, or add information to an existing conversation. For creating new items, use create_issue, create_discussion, or create_pull_request instead. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 2 comment(s) can be added. Target: *. Supports reply_to_id for discussion threading.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"body":{"description":"The comment text in Markdown format. This is the 'body' field - do not use 'comment_body' or other variations. Provide helpful, relevant information that adds value to the conversation.","type":"string"},"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"item_number":{"description":"The issue, pull request, or discussion number to comment on. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 123 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/123). Can also be a temporary_id (e.g., 'aw_abc123') from a previously created issue in the same workflow run. If omitted, the tool will attempt to resolve the target from the current workflow context (triggering issue, PR, or discussion). If auto-targeting fails (e.g., for schedule or workflow_dispatch triggers), the tool call will fail with an error.","type":["number","string"]},"reply_to_id":{"description":"Node ID of the discussion comment to reply to, enabling threaded discussion comments. When provided, the new comment is posted as a reply to the specified top-level discussion comment. If the given node ID belongs to a nested reply, the handler automatically resolves it to the top-level parent. Only applicable for discussion comments — ignored for issue and pull request comments.","type":"string"},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"},"temporary_id":{"description":"Unique temporary identifier for this comment. Format: 'aw_' followed by 3 to 12 alphanumeric characters (e.g., 'aw_abc1', 'aw_Test123'). Auto-generated if not provided. This field is required — omitting it will cause a validation error.","items":{"type":"string"},"type":"array"},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"}},"required":["labels"],"type":"object"},"name":"add_labels"},{"description":"Remove labels from an existing GitHub issue or pull request. 2026-04-27T15:23:40.1739836Z {"timestamp":"2026-04-27T15:23:19.833915903Z","direction":"IN","type":"RESPONSE","server_id":"github","payload":{"id":1,"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"content":[{"text":"{\"number\":38300,\"title\":\"SecretClient.setSecret() silently drops contentType option — never sent to REST API\",\"body\":\"- **Package Name**: `@azure/keyvault-secrets`\\n- **Package Version**: `4.11.1`\\n- **Operating system**: Linux (confirmed in Azure Kubernetes Service pod)\\n- [x] **nodejs**\\n    - **version**: \\u0026gt;=24.0.0\\n- [ ] **browser**\\n    - **name/version**:\\n- [x] **typescript**\\n    - **version**: (fill in from your `tsconfig` / `package.json`)\\n- Is the bug related to **documentation** in\\n  - [ ] README.md\\n  - [ ] source code documentation\\n  - [ ] SDK API docs on https://learn.microsoft.com\\n\\n**Describe the bug**\\n\\n`SetSecretOptions` declares a `contentType` property, but it is silently dropped and never included in the outgoing REST request body. In `dist/commonjs/index.js`, `setSecret()` destructures only `enabled`, `notBefore`, `expiresOn`, and `tags` from options. `contentType` falls into `...remainingOptions`, which is forwarded to the tracing client only ‚Äî not to the body serializer:\\n\\n```javascript\\n// SecretClient.setSecret() ‚Äî dist/commonjs/index.js\\nsetSecret(secretName, value, options = {}) {\\n  const { enabled, notBefore, expiresOn: expires, tags, ...remainingOptions } = options;\\n  return tracingClient.withSpan(\\u0026#34;SecretClient.setSecret\\u0026#34;, remainingOptions, async (updatedOptions) =\\u0026gt; {\\n    const response = await this.client.setSecret(\\n      secretName,\\n      { value, secretAttributes: { enabled, notBefore, expires }, tags }, // ‚Üê contentType absent\\n      updatedOptions\\n    );\\n  });\\n}\\n```\\n\\nThe body serializer in `dist/commonjs/models/models.js` does handle `contentType` correctly ‚Äî it just never receives it:\\n\\n```javascript\\nfunction secretSetParametersSerializer(item) {\\n  return {\\n    value: item[\\u0026#34;value\\u0026#34;],\\n    tags: item[\\u0026#34;tags\\u0026#34;],\\n    contentType: item[\\u0026#34;contentType\\u0026#34;], // this would work if item contained it\\n    attributes: secretAttributesSerializer(item[\\u0026#34;secretAttributes\\u0026#34;])\\n  };\\n}\\n```\\n\\nThe same bug exists in `updateSecretProperties()`, making it impossible to use as a post-creation workaround:\\n\\n```javascript\\n// SecretClient.updateSecretProperties() ‚Äî dist/commonjs/index.js\\nasync updateSecretProperties(secretName, secretVersion, options = {}) {\\n  const { enabled, notBefore, expiresOn: expires, tags, ...remainingOptions } = options;\\n  return tracingClient.withSpan(\\u0026#34;SecretClient.updateSecretProperties\\u0026#34;, remainingOptions, async (updatedOptions) =\\u0026gt; {\\n    const response = await this.client.updateSecret(\\n      secretName,\\n      secretVersion,\\n      { secretAttributes: { enabled, notBefore, expires }, tags }, // contentType absent here too\\n      updatedOptions\\n    );\\n  });\\n}\\n```\\n\\n**To Reproduce**\\n\\nSteps to reproduce the behavior:\\n1. Create a `SecretClient` and call `setSecret` with a `contentType` option:\\n    ```typescript\\n    const client = new SecretClient(vaultUrl, credential);\\n    await client.setSecret(\\u0026#39;my-secret\\u0026#39;, \\u0026#39;my-value\\u0026#39;, { contentType: \\u0026#39;text/plain\\u0026#39; });\\n    ```\\n2. Intercept the outgoing HTTP request (e.g. via a pipeline policy) or inspect the secret in Azure Portal after creation.\\n3. Observe that `contentType` is absent from the request body and not set on the resulting secret.\\n\\nA concrete consequence: Azure Key Vault policies enforcing `\\u0026#34;Secrets should have content type set\\u0026#34;` (policy definition id `75262d3e-ba4a-4f43-85f8-9f72c090e5e3`) will block the `setSecret` call entirely, even when `contentType` is explicitly passed in options.\\n\\n**Expected behavior**\\n\\n`contentType: \\u0026#39;text/plain\\u0026#39;` passed in `SetSecretOptions` should be included in the `PUT /secrets/{name}` request body, resulting in the property being set on the created/updated secret version.\\n\\n**Screenshots**\\n\\nN/A\\n\\n**Additional context**\\n\\nA workaround is to inject a pipeline policy at `SecretClient` construction that writes `contentType` directly into the request body:\\n\\n```typescript\\nconst policy = {\\n  name: \\u0026#39;ensureSecretContentType\\u0026#39;,\\n  async sendRequest(request, next) {\\n    if (request.method === \\u0026#39;PUT\\u0026#39; \\u0026amp;\\u0026amp; /\\\\/secrets\\\\/[^/?]+/.test(request.url)) {\\n      const body = JSON.parse(request.body || \\u0026#39;{}\\u0026#39;);\\n      if (!body.contentType) {\\n        body.contentType = \\u0026#39;text/plain\\u0026#39;;\\n        request.body = JSON.stringify(body);\\n      }\\n    }\\n    return next(request);\\n  }\\n};\\n\\nconst client = new SecretClient(vaultUrl, credential, {\\n  additionalPolicies: [{ policy, position: \\u0026#39;perCall\\u0026#39; }]\\n});\\n```\\n\",\"state\":\"open\",\"html_url\":\"https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-js/issues/38300\",\"user\":{\"login\":\"aqbpiercey\",\"id\":105325785,\"profile_url\":\"https://github.com/aqbpiercey\",\"avatar_url\":\"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/105325785?v=4\"},\"author_association\":\"NONE\",\"labels\":[\"question\",\"customer-reported\",\"needs-triage\"],\"reactions\":{\"total_count\":1,\"+1\":0,\"-1\":0,\"laugh\":0,\"confused\":0,\"heart\":0,\"hooray\":0,\"rocket\":0,\"eyes\":1},\"created_at\":\"2026-04-27T15:21:17Z\",\"updated_at\":\"2026-04-27T15:21:49Z\"}","type":"text"}]}}}
2026-04-27T15:23:40.1757013Z {"timestamp":"2026-04-27T15:23:19.846143038Z","direction":"OUT","type":"REQUEST","server_id":"github","method":"tools/call","payload":{"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"tools/call","params":{"arguments":{"issue_number":38300,"method":"get","owner":"Azure","repo":"azure-sdk-for-js"},"name":"issue_read"}}}

{\\n    const response = await this.client.setSecret(\\n      secretName,\\n      { value, secretAttributes: { enabled, notBefore, expires }, tags }, // ‚Üê contentType absent\\n      updatedOptions\\n    );\\n  });\\n}\\n```\\n\\nThe body serializer in `dist/commonjs/models/models.js` does handle `contentType` correctly ‚Äî it just never receives it:\\n\\n```javascript\\nfunction secretSetParametersSerializer(item) {\\n  return {\\n    value: item[\\u0026#34;value\\u0026#34;],\\n    tags: item[\\u0026#34;tags\\u0026#34;],\\n    contentType: item[\\u0026#34;contentType\\u0026#34;], // this would work if item contained it\\n    attributes: secretAttributesSerializer(item[\\u0026#34;secretAttributes\\u0026#34;])\\n  };\\n}\\n```\\n\\nThe same bug exists in `updateSecretProperties()`, making it impossible to use as a post-creation workaround:\\n\\n```javascript\\n// SecretClient.updateSecretProperties() ‚Äî dist/commonjs/index.js\\nasync updateSecretProperties(secretName, secretVersion, options = {}) {\\n  const { enabled, notBefore, expiresOn: expires, tags, ...remainingOptions } = options;\\n  return tracingClient.withSpan(\\u0026#34;SecretClient.updateSecretProperties\\u0026#34;, remainingOptions, async (updatedOptions) =\\u0026gt; {\\n    const response = await this.client.updateSecret(\\n      secretName,\\n      secretVersion,\\n      { secretAttributes: { enabled, notBefore, expires }, tags }, // contentType absent here too\\n      updatedOptions\\n    );\\n  });\\n}\\n```\\n\\n**To Reproduce**\\n\\nSteps to reproduce the behavior:\\n1. Intercept the outgoing HTTP request (e.g. via a pipeline policy) or inspect the secret in Azure Portal after creation.\\n3. Observe that `contentType` is absent from the request body and not set on the resulting secret.\\n\\nA concrete consequence: Azure Key Vault policies enforcing `\\u0026#34;Secrets should have content type set\\u0026#34;` (policy definition id `75262d3e-ba4a-4f43-85f8-9f72c090e5e3`) will block the `setSecret` call entirely, even when `contentType` is explicitly passed in options.\\n\\n**Expected behavior**\\n\\n`contentType: \\u0026#39;text/plain\\u0026#39;` passed in `SetSecretOptions` should be included in the `PUT /secrets/{name}` request body, resulting in the property being set on the created/updated secret version.\\n\\n**Screenshots**\\n\\nN/A\\n\\n**Additional context**\\n\\nA workaround is to inject a pipeline policy at `SecretClient` construction that writes `contentType` directly into the request body:\\n\\n```typescript\\nconst policy = {\\n  name: \\u0026#39;ensureSecretContentType\\u0026#39;,\\n  async sendRequest(request, next) {\\n    if (request.method === \\u0026#39;PUT\\u0026#39;
